segments.text: Job replied,
How long will you go on tormenting me? How long will you go on crushing me with words?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Ten times already you have humiliated me. Aren't you ashamed for treating me so badly?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Even if I did sin, that's my problem, and has nothing to do with you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You think you're so much better than me, and you use my degradation against me.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But you should realize that it's God who has wronged me, [^111] he has trapped me in his net.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Even though I cry for help, I get no answer; even though I shout my objections, I get no justice.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: God has walled me in so I can't escape; he has plunged my path into darkness.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He has stripped my honor[^112] from me; he has taken away my reputation. [^113]
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He tears me down from all sides until I am finished; he has destroyed my hope like a tree that is uprooted.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: His anger burns against me; he treats me as one of his enemies.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: God's troops assemble to attack me. They build ramparts against me. They encircle and besiege my home.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He has driven my brothers far away from me; all my former friends are estranged from me.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: My relatives have abandoned me; my close friends have forgotten me.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: My house guests and my maidservants treat me as a stranger—to them I have become a foreigner.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I call my servant, but he doesn't reply. I have to beg him!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I[^114] am repulsive to my wife, and I am loathsome to my own brothers. [^115]
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Even young children despise me; when I st and up they ridicule me.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: All my closest friends despise me, and those I loved have turned on me.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I've been reduced to skin and bones and I survive by the skin of my teeth.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Have pity on me, my friends, have pity on me, because God has struck me down!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Why are you persecuting me like God does? Aren't you satisfied with getting your pound of flesh?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I wish my words could be written down, recorded in a book,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: or engraved with an iron pen and molten lead in the rock forever.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I know my Redeemer is alive, and that he shall finally take the st and[^116] for me on the earth.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Even though my skin is destroyed, in my body[^117] I shall see God.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I myself will see him—with my own eyes, and not those of someone else! The thought overcomes me! [^118]
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You say to yourselves, ‘How can we make him suffer so he can see he is the source of his problems?’
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You yourselves should fear being punished by God, for you know anger brings God's punishment[^119] that accompanies judgment.
segments.speaker: Job
